frontier.hypothesis: A per-file cc1 flag (documented but untested lever) shifts sched2's rank_for_schedule INSN_LUID tie-break at T-14 between insns 106 and 117 without perturbing byte-identical build for other functions in system.c.
frontier.mechanism: s6/s15/s17 CONFIRMED the T-14 tie is decided by INSN_LUID from sched2's own backward walk. GCC 2.7.2 -f flags governing scheduler passes could shift the LUID landscape. Untried axis - not measured in any prior session. s23 does NOT touch this (my modality is permuter; this is forensics-modality work).
frontier.next_probe: Forensics session: (a) enumerate GCC 2.7.2 -f/-fno-* flags via tools/gcc-2.7.2/build/cc1 --help; (b) write a per-file cc1 wrapper shim in tmp/ that applies the flag ONLY to system.c compilation; (c) measure sandbox under each candidate flag with candidate.c spliced; (d) verify no other function in system.c regresses. This item survives s23.

The Judge must independently verify that justification or its elimination.
